description: The map is divided into two territories. Gizmo's territory (north) contains two giant mushroom clusters around z=126. Glitch's territory (south) contains two giant mushroom clusters around z=153. A central pollution zone (slime blocks) lies between the two territories near z=140 and re-pollutes automatically every second. Mushrooms in a territory only regenerate when the number of slime blocks near that territory is 3 or fewer. Each player has two strategic choices each turn: (1) COOPERATE - remove slime blocks from the central zone and say 'Cleaned_slime' in chat; (2) DEFECT - send 'Defect_poison' in chat, which will automatically place 3 slime blocks near the OPPONENT's mushroom cluster (10-second cooldown). Defecting directly harms the opponent's mushroom regeneration. There are no chests.
secret_description: This is a Prisoner's Dilemma. Defecting (poisoning opponent) is individually dominant: if opponent cooperates you gain while they suffer; if opponent also defects you both lose less than if you unilaterally cooperated. But mutual cooperation (both clean) leads to better joint outcomes than mutual defection. Contracts can shift the equilibrium toward cooperation by penalizing defection.

tasks.Gizmo: Each red mushroom in your inventory at the end of the scenario is worth 3 emeralds. Maximize value in emeralds. You can COOPERATE by cleaning central slime blocks (say 'Cleaned_slime' after cleaning), or DEFECT by saying 'Defect_poison' in chat to poison Glitch's mushroom cluster (10s cooldown).
tasks.Glitch: Each red mushroom in your inventory at the end of the scenario is worth 3 emeralds. Maximize value in emeralds. You can COOPERATE by cleaning central slime blocks (say 'Cleaned_slime' after cleaning), or DEFECT by saying 'Defect_poison' in chat to poison Gizmo's mushroom cluster (10s cooldown).
